answer:
Ramp uses the OAuth 2.0 framework for API authentication [1][2][3]. To access the API, you must first obtain an access token by requesting it from the /developer/v1/token endpoint [1][4]. When requesting this token, you authenticate your application using your Client ID and Client Secret via HTTP Basic Auth [5][4]. The base64-encoded string of client_id:client_secret is typically sent in the Authorization header [5][4]. Once you have your access token, you must include it in the header of your API requests as a Bearer token [4][3].

```http
Authorization: Bearer <YOUR_ACCESS_TOKEN>
```

For token acquisition requests, the authentication header is formatted as follows [5][4]:

```http
Authorization: Basic <BASE64_ENCODED_CLIENT_ID:CLIENT_SECRET>
```
